activities_projects.title: Design a Working Electric Alarm
activities_projects.objective: Apply electromagnet concepts to build a functional device
steps: Create an electromagnet by wrapping copper wire around an iron nail
Connect it to a battery and metal strip
Arrange the strip so it repeatedly touches and breaks the circuit
Observe the repeated striking sound like an alarm
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands how electromagnets are used in devices like alarms and bells
activities_projects.evaluation: Functionality and explanation of the model (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Electromagnet Strength Investigation
steps: Wrap the nail with different numbers of wire turns
Connect to battery and test how many paper clips it lifts
Record results for each trial
Analyze the relationship between turns and strength
activities_projects.evaluation: Accuracy of data and interpretation (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Build a Mini Electric Heater Model
activities_projects.objective: Understand heating effect of electric current
steps: Connect nichrome wire in a simple electric circuit
Close the switch to allow current flow
Observe heating in the wire
Explain how heating effect is used in appliances
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands the heating effect of current in real devices

activities_projects.title: Investigating Electrical Resistance
activities_projects.objective: Explore how different materials affect electric current
steps: Create circuits using wires of different thickness and length
Observe brightness of the bulb
Compare the results
Infer how resistance changes
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands resistance and current flow concepts
activities_projects.evaluation: Quality of comparison and conclusion (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Magnetic Field Mapping
activities_projects.objective: Visualize magnetic field around an electromagnet
steps: Create an electromagnet using a nail and copper wire
Place paper over the magnet
Sprinkle iron filings
Observe patterns formed

activities_projects.title: Short Circuit Investigation
activities_projects.objective: Understand dangers of improper circuit connections
steps: Create a correct circuit and observe bulb glow
Connect wires directly across battery without load
Observe heating of wires
Discuss why short circuits are dangerous

activities_projects.title: Electromagnet vs Permanent Magnet Comparison
steps: Test lifting ability of both magnets
Switch off the electromagnet
Observe differences in behavior
Record observations
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands differences between electromagnets and permanent magnets

activities_projects.title: Design a Circuit with Multiple Bulbs
activities_projects.objective: Explore how current flows in complex circuits
steps: Design a circuit connecting two bulbs
Observe brightness when both bulbs are connected
Disconnect one bulb and observe changes
Explain current distribution
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Develops understanding of current flow in circuits

activities_projects.title: Energy Consumption Investigation
activities_projects.objective: Understand electricity use in household appliances
steps: List electrical appliances used at home
Record their power ratings
Compare which devices use more electricity
Suggest ways to reduce energy use
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Applies electricity concepts to real life

activities_projects.title: Design an Electric Device Prototype
activities_projects.objective: Encourage creative application of electricity concepts
steps: Design a small device using an electric circuit
Explain how electricity makes it work
Demonstrate the device
Present the idea to classmates
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Develops creativity and practical understanding of circuits
